title: Master_Recycling_Guide_Part_7_Textiles_Bulky_Items
text:
------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Scrap Metal

Many recycling centers accept:

-   Bicycles
-   Metal shelving
-   Aluminum ladders
-   Metal pipes
-   Metal fencing
-   Filing cabinets

Size limits vary by facility.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Household Goods

Donate usable:

-   Dishes
-   Pots
-   Pans
-   Toys
-   Sporting goods
-   Baby equipment (if permitted)
-   Books

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Holiday Items

### Christmas Lights

Recycle through specialty electronics or holiday light recycling
programs.

### Artificial Trees

Usually not recyclable curbside.

### Natural Trees

Many communities offer seasonal composting or mulch programs.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Special Collection Events

Communities may host collection events for:

-   Electronics
-   Batteries
-   Paint
-   Household hazardous waste
-   Paper shredding
-   Tires
-   Mattresses

Check your local government website for schedules.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Frequently Asked Questions

### Can I recycle clothes?

Usually not in curbside recycling. Donate or use textile recycling.

### Can I recycle shoes?

Use donation or shoe recycling programs.

### Can I recycle a couch?

Schedule bulky-item collection or donate if usable.

### Can I recycle a mattress?

Use a mattress recycling program where available.

### Can I recycle a bicycle?

Many scrap metal recyclers accept bicycles.

### Can I recycle a refrigerator?

Yes through appliance recycling programs.

### Can I recycle tires?

Use tire retailers or approved collection sites.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
